name: ETH Value Recovery Scale-In v1
edge_mechanism: scale-in around value recovery when confirmation improves across bars instead of one-shot all-in timing
thesis: ETH 1h is the best control lane for testing whether value recovery after a shallow displacement can be traded more robustly by scaling in as confirmation improves across bars rather than committing everything on the first reclaim. The edge should come from denser participation around repaired value, not from exact bottom-picking.
invalidation_condition: Abort if value-recovery signals remain too sparse, if scale-in improves entry quality but still cannot overcome chop, or if the setup simply accumulates into dead bounces.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price displaces away from fast value, begins reclaiming it, confirms the reclaim over multiple bars, and then rotates far enough back through value for a tradable recovery leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In chop, the first reclaim bar is often noisy, but multi-bar improvement around value can capture denser recovery moves while reducing all-in timing risk.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 1h is the best lane for a recovery-scale-in test because it should provide enough signal frequency without the microstructure instability of thinner alts.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Price may reclaim value often but fail to extend beyond it, turning the scale-in structure into churn rather than edge.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries are dense but expectancy is weak, shorten holding time and front-load profit extraction before changing the scale-in grammar.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: More frequent trades than prior sparse families, with modest first-target captures carrying most expectancy.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: ETH 1h and one cross-lane show real density and positive post-cost recovery after multi-bar value reclaim.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, low-trade mirage, or repeated value reclaims that cannot rotate far enough after entry.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; displacement, reclaim, and directional improvement are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: explicitly reduced by multi-bar confirmation and scale-in design

validation_basket.why_included: Best control lane for dense value-recovery scale-in testing.
validation_basket.continue_if: Reclaim sequences trade often enough and recover far enough after costs.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Reclaims are dense but fail to travel, or remain sparse anyway.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Frequent repaired-value rotations with modest but repeatable recovery travel.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same value-recovery mechanism survives on a similar major lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC preserves the recovery-scale-in shape with acceptable density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The mechanism only works on ETH-specific intraday structure.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Slightly cleaner but less frequent recovery rotations than ETH.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same mechanism generalizes to a slower adjacent timeframe.
validation_basket.continue_if: 4h preserves the reclaim structure without collapsing trade count.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but cleaner value recovery trades than 1h.

long: Exit remaining 25% on close through EMA_55 or 3 bars
short: Exit remaining 25% on close through EMA_55 or 3 bars
